primary_method: component unit NAV with filing-verified ETH look-through and additive trajectory net of dilution
weighting_rationale: BMNR is routed as scarce_asset_optionality with primary method component_owner_cash_and_unit_nav. The economic claim is a diluted common share of an Ethereum treasury vehicle, not recurring operating owner cash. Filing-verified static ETH look-through (~$18.76 per share at May 31, 2026 on 5,416,945 ETH and 579.7 million shares) is the decision-grade anchor because it is primary-verified, overlap-controlled, and separated from the eth_trajectory_net_dilution row. That component schedule dominates because it matches the levered-treasury payoff lens and maps directly to the 10-Q digital asset table. Marks credit-cycle review is a financing-cycle survivability and dilution stress test ($340.3M cash, no term debt, but 149% nine-month share growth and $287.6M operating cash use); it bounds equity-stub risk but does not replace unit NAV. Klarman asset-realization audit confirms no dated liquidation, buyback, or distribution catalyst, so passive mNAV normalization is corroboration only, not a competing primary value. Comparable-transaction and replacement-cost corroborators are silent: no indexed arm's-length crypto-treasury liquidation precedents appear in the frozen packet. Lawrence yield-curve base return (6.3% per year over seven years) remains the stance gate, not a second asset stack. The chair does not average static look-through ($18.76), the challenged trajectory uplift (+$4.50 base), and the component-low financing stress total ($8.50) into a blended consensus.
agreed_facts: At May 31, 2026, BMNR held 5,416,945 ETH at fair value $10.86 billion plus 203 BTC at $15 million; common shares outstanding were 579.7 million versus 232.4 million at August 31, 2025.
Filing ETH fair value per share was ~$18.76 at May 31, 2026, exceeding the frozen market price of $15.69 (roughly 0.84x mNAV on May marks).
Nine-month general and administrative expense was $335.7 million against $56.9 million of staking and validation revenue; net cash used in operating activities was $287.6 million.
Cash was $340.3 million at May 31, 2026; the company carries no material operating term debt at scale in the frozen filings.

Deferred tax liability was $92.3 million (~$0.16 per share on 579.7 million shares) and warrant liability was $2.1 million; BMNP preferred stock claims are flagged but not fully modeled in the senior-claim reserve row.

Component overlap controls are clean: eth_look_through_today, eth_trajectory_net_dilution, staking_and_legacy_cash, and warrants_tax_preferred_reserve use distinct overlap keys with no double-count of static ETH with trajectory uplift.

Observed issuance cadence (149% share increase in roughly nine months) contradicts the eth_trajectory_net_dilution base assumption of 10% cumulative ATM dilution over seven years; the targeted research loop confirmed this mismatch.
Round-two votes split one watch (hk) and two defer (klarman_asset_value, marks_credit_cycle); no rater recommended add, buy, hold-for-sizing, or pass.
disputed_facts: Whether the ~16% discount to May ETH marks is mispricing or a rational price for an equity-financed dilution vehicle where ATM issuance can outrun token appreciation.
Whether the eth_trajectory_net_dilution base row (+$4.50 per share) belongs in the primary payoff build given filing-contradicted dilution cadence and null success_probability.
BMNP preferred conversion terms, liquidation preference, and post-May 2026 ATM issuance pace needed to bound common look-through after senior claims.
Whether live ETH spot versus May 31, 2026 fair value marks materially changes static look-through before any capital decision.
Whether management alignment, strategic advisor warrant economics, and Tom Lee chairman incentives support a slowdown in dilution (management_facts document_count is zero in the frozen packet).
strongest_dissent: Klarman asset-realization and Marks credit-cycle cross-checks: the market may rationally price BMNR as an open-ended equity-financing cycle where management funds ETH purchases by issuing shares faster than ETH per diluted share compounds, making the apparent NAV discount a financing-vehicle trap rather than catalyst-backed margin of safety; near-term cash supports survivability but does not bound dilution or preferred seniority.

dissent_ledger.issue: No dated catalyst converts treasury ETH into per-share cash; post-May ATM and preferred waterfall remain unresolved
Equity-financing cycle dominates: 149% nine-month share growth contradicts base dilution path; senior-claim waterfall incomplete
Scarce ETH stack is real but not a bounded common claim until dilution pace and preferred seniority are proven

value_range_per_share.low_source: component schedule total (ETH mark haircut, trajectory stress, and financing-cycle dilution)
value_range_per_share.base_source: filing-verified ETH fair value per share at May 31, 2026 (10-Q digital asset table; before unmodeled preferred claims)
value_range_per_share.high_source: component schedule base total if eth_trajectory_net_dilution assumptions held (not filing-evidenced after research loop)

expected_return_range_pct.note: Base reflects Lawrence yield-curve stance gate at $15.69. Low bound reflects component-low annualized path and financing-stress framing. High bound requires bull scenario with limited further dilution. Range is not averaged across methods.

entry_range_per_share.condition: No new capital commitment until post-May 2026 ATM issuance is reconciled to ETH per diluted share and a decision-grade BMNP preferred-and-warrant waterfall bounds common look-through. Nominal discount to May ETH marks alone is insufficient entry evidence.

recommendation_rationale: Two defer and one watch across three independent groups. The primary component unit-NAV method shows filing-verified static ETH look-through above price, but base annualized return (6.3% per year) sits well below a mid-teens accumulate hurdle, the eth_trajectory additive row is filing-contradicted, G&A runs roughly six times staking revenue, and BMNP preferred plus post-May dilution remain unresolved after one targeted research loop. This is a monitoring stance pending senior-claim reconciliation and dilution cadence evidence, not a sizing or capital decision.

operational_milestones: Reconcile quarter-by-quarter ETH purchases, shares issued, and ETH per diluted share from May 31, 2026 forward
Publish a per-share waterfall netting BMNP preferred, warrant, and deferred-tax claims against ETH look-through
Track ATM, private placement, and warrant exercise proceeds versus ETH accumulation and G&A burn
Compare staking and validation revenue to general and administrative expense and operating cash flow each quarter
Monitor live ETH spot versus May 31, 2026 fair value marks and mNAV ratio
Scan 8-K and shelf filings for dilutive equity raises, preferred conversions, or treasury policy changes
Audit custody, validator, and counterparty exposures against 10-K risk factors
valuation_refresh_triggers: Next 10-Q or 10-K filing with updated ETH inventory and share count
Two consecutive quarters where ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises
BMNP preferred conversion or material warrant exercise
ETH fair value per share move exceeding 20% from May 31, 2026 marks without corresponding filing update
Management announcement of buyback, distribution, or dated treasury liquidation program
20% price move without corresponding filing update

price_review_thresholds.action: Re-underwrite component-low financing stress if dilution continues; do not add on price alone
Require confirmed dilution slowdown and preferred waterfall before treating mNAV gap as closed
Reassess whether component base trajectory assumptions are filing-supported or fully priced

thesis_break_conditions: ATM issuance dilutes ETH per share faster than token price rises for two consecutive quarters
Preferred or warrant conversion materially reduces common look-through below the component low case ($8.50 total)
ETH fair value per share falls below the low case after sustained spot decline and further dilution
Corporate G&A and operating cash burn exceed staking inflow with no equity issuance for two reporting periods (liquidity stress)
Management abandons ETH treasury strategy or pivots capital away from token accumulation without shareholder distribution
